evals.description: The skill must ask for destination before analysis or mutation when destination is absent.
evals.prompt: Create tracked stories for the product brief in `product-brief.md`.

assertions.prompt: The response should ask the user to choose a destination tracker (GitHub Issues, Linear, or Agile Accelerator) before creating stories or doing deep analysis.
The response should not claim that issues, records, labels, or work items were created because the destination was not chosen.

evals.description: After GitHub destination is chosen for a codebase/ideas source, the router hands off to arc-creating-user-stories and its story contract.
evals.prompt: Destination: GitHub Issues. Draft tracked work items for `product-brief.md`, but do not create issues until I approve.

assertions.prompt: The response should state that it is continuing with the arc-creating-user-stories skill (or its STORY_FORMAT contract) for GitHub Issues backlog creation, rather than inventing its own story format.

Any drafted work items should use a job-story sentence ('When ..., I want ..., so that ...') and checklist acceptance criteria where each criterion names a verification method (a test, command, or observable check). Given/When/Then Gherkin scenarios should not be used.
The response should not claim that GitHub issues were created because the user asked for a draft before approval.

evals.description: Existing issue implementation planning belongs to arc-planning-work.
evals.prompt: Issue #42 already exists. Create an implementation plan for it; do not create any new stories or work items.

assertions.prompt: The response should not create or draft new stories/work items; it should recognize this as implementation planning for existing work.
